title: Custom-code path extras and overrides
description: composer.json's extra.installer-paths is the canonical source for where custom modules / themes / profiles live; this file declares only the surfaces composer doesn't know about (drush commands, repo docs, workflow YAML) plus optional overrides for non-standard layouts. Globs use picomatch / minimatch syntax — the same patterns cspell, eslint, and markdownlint-cli2 already accept.

$schema.description: Pointer to this schema for editor validation

_comment.description: Free-form note explaining this file's role; ignored by tools

extras.description: Custom-code surfaces composer.json doesn't describe — drush commands, repo docs, workflow YAML, etc. Append-only by design: downstream sites add their own surfaces here without touching upstream defaults.

custom_drush.description: Custom drush command paths (the team writes directly under drush/Commands/, distinct from contrib drush installed at drush/Commands/contrib/)

custom_php_other.description: PHP files outside installer-paths (e.g. root-level helpers, custom integration code at src/integrations/)

custom_js_other.description: JS files outside installer-paths (e.g. build scripts under scripts/)

custom_md.description: Upstream-authored markdown for markdownlint coverage (README, CHANGELOG, contributor docs, etc.)

custom_workflows.description: GitHub Actions workflows and composite actions (for actionlint coverage)

installer_paths_overrides.description: Escape hatch for sites that organize themes / modules / profiles outside what composer.json's installer-paths cover (e.g. a theme committed directly at web/themes/myorg/ without a corresponding type:drupal-custom-theme installer entry). Empty by default. Avoid using this if you can update composer.json instead.

exclude.description: Glob patterns excluded from every form of discovery — vendor/, node_modules/, contrib code, Drupal core. Always applied regardless of whether a path is in extras or matched via installer-paths or autodiscovery.

info_yml_autodiscovery.description: When true (default), the orchestrator scans for *.info.yml files outside exclude paths and treats their parent directories as additional custom modules / themes — a safety net for sites that committed custom code without updating composer.json or this file. Set to false for strict allowlist-only behavior.
